001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.AnnotationTraversal.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ClassUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
026
027import java.lang.annotation.*;
028import java.lang.reflect.*;
029import java.net.*;
030import java.util.*;
031import java.util.function.*;
032import java.util.stream.*;
033
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
038
039/**
040 * Contains metadata about a bean property.
041 *
042 * <p>
043 * Contains information such as type of property (e.g. field/getter/setter), class type of property value, and whether
044 * any transforms are associated with this property.
045 *
046 * <p>
047 * Developers will typically not need access to this class.  The information provided by it is already exposed through
048 * several methods on the {@link BeanMap} API.
049 *
050 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
051 *
052 * Instances are immutable after construction and are safe for concurrent read access.
053 *
054 */
055@SuppressWarnings({
056   "rawtypes", // Raw types necessary for generic type handling
057   "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
058   "java:S115",   // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_field, ARG_value)
059   "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - BeanMap<?>, ClassMeta<?> for property metadata
060})
061public class BeanPropertyMeta implements Comparable<BeanPropertyMeta> {
062
063   /**
064    * Sentinel value that, when returned from {@link BeanPropertyMeta.Builder#writeTransform} during
065    * {@link #set(BeanMap, String, Object)}, causes the set to be skipped entirely — neither the configured setter
066    * is invoked nor the property cache updated.
067    *
068    * <p>
069    * Provided as a generic SPI hook so the marshalling layer (or any other transform-installing extension) can
070    * implement "leave the bean's pre-existing value in place" semantics — used by the per-property null-coercion
071    * {@code SKIP} mode added to the marshalling layer in 10.0.0.
072    *
073    * @since 10.0.0
074    */
075   public static final Object SKIP_VALUE = new Object();
076
077   // Property name constants
078   private static final String PROP_field = "field";
079   private static final String PROP_getter = "getter";
080   private static final String PROP_name = "name";
081   private static final String PROP_setter = "setter";
082   private static final String PROP_type = "type";
083
084   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
085   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
086   private static final String ARG_innerField = "innerField";
087
088   // Error message constants
089   private static final String MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined = "Getter or public field not defined on property ''{0}''";
090
091   /**
092    * BeanPropertyMeta builder class.
093    */
094   @SuppressWarnings({
095      "java:S1104" // Public mutable fields are an intentional cross-module SPI seam: the marshalling-side post-processor (different package) reads/writes them after validate(), so they cannot be final or package-private.
096   })
097   public static class Builder {
098      BeanMeta<?> beanMeta;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
099      BeanTypeResolver bc;  // The bean-modeling SPI seam to the marshalling-side type resolver.  Null when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path.
100      BeanConfigContext config;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access.  Always non-null — sourced from the owning BeanMeta.
101      public String name;  // Public so marshalling-side post-processors can use it when constructing diagnostic messages (e.g. MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor's schema-validation transform).
102      FieldInfo field;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
103      public FieldInfo innerField;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
104      public MethodInfo getter;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
105      public MethodInfo setter;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
106      MethodInfo extraKeys;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access
107      public BeanInfo<?> rawTypeMeta;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access (used to install swap-aware transforms).  Null on commons-side path (no type resolution).  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta} since it's the only in-tree implementation; the field is typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam so the field can live in commons.bean.
108      public Object swap;  // Object-typed so the field can live in commons.bean; cast to ObjectSwap by marshalling-side consumers.  Set only via MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor (marshalling-side post-processor).
109      public BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> readTransform;  // Package-private; defaults to identity if null.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam; marshalling-side installers cast the session argument back to {@code MarshallingSession} where needed (see {@code MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor#installSwapAwareTransforms}).
110      public BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> writeTransform; // Package-private; defaults to identity if null.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam (see readTransform note).
111      public List<ClassInfo> dictionaryClasses;  // Package-private for BeanMeta access; @MarshalledProp(dictionary={}) classes scanned during validate().
112      public Set<String> views;  // Named views this property belongs to; null = untagged (in all views when defaultViewInclusion is enabled). Set via MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor.
113      private boolean isConstructorArg;
114      public boolean isUri;  // Package-private so MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor can set @Uri-derived flag.  Mirrors rawTypeMeta.isUri() plus @Uri annotation reads on field/getter/setter.
115      private boolean isDyna;
116      private boolean isDynaGetterMap;
117      public BeanInfo<?> typeMeta;  // Package-private so the marshalling-side post-processor can override after @Swap/@MarshalledProp detection.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
118      private Object overrideValue;
119      private BeanPropertyMeta delegateFor;
120      private boolean canRead;
121      private boolean canWrite;
122      private boolean readOnly;
123      private boolean writeOnly;
124
125      Builder(BeanMeta<?> beanMeta, String name) {
126         this.beanMeta = beanMeta;
127         this.bc = beanMeta.getBeanTypeResolver();  // Returns null on commons-side path.
128         this.config = beanMeta.getConfig();
129         this.name = name;
130      }
131
132      /**
133       * @return A new BeanPropertyMeta object using this builder.
134       */
135      public BeanPropertyMeta build() {
136         return new BeanPropertyMeta(this);
137      }
138
139      /**
140       * Sets the original bean property that this one is overriding.
141       *
142       * @param value The original bean property that this one is overriding.
143       * @return This object.
144       */
145      public Builder delegateFor(BeanPropertyMeta value) {
146         delegateFor = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
147         return this;
148      }
149
150      /**
151       * Installs the read-side value transform for this property.
152       *
153       * <p>
154       * Applied to the raw getter result by {@link BeanPropertyMeta#get(BeanMap,String)} before it is returned to the
155       * caller.  Defaults to identity (raw value passes through unchanged).
156       *
157       * <p>
158       * Used by the marshalling layer to install {@code ObjectSwap}-aware behavior at bean-meta construction time;
159       * the bean-modeling layer itself only invokes the function and does not directly reference
160       * {@code ObjectSwap}.
161       *
162       * @param value The transform function.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
163       * @return This object.
164       */
165      public Builder readTransform(BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> value) {
166         readTransform = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
167         return this;
168      }
169
170      /**
171       * Installs the write-side value transform for this property.
172       *
173       * <p>
174       * Applied to the incoming value by {@link BeanPropertyMeta#set(BeanMap,String,Object)} before the raw setter is
175       * invoked.  Defaults to identity (raw value passes through unchanged).
176       *
177       * <p>
178       * Used by the marshalling layer to install {@code ObjectSwap}-aware behavior at bean-meta construction time;
179       * the bean-modeling layer itself only invokes the function and does not directly reference
180       * {@code ObjectSwap}.
181       *
182       * @param value The transform function.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
183       * @return This object.
184       */
185      public Builder writeTransform(BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> value) {
186         writeTransform = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
187         return this;
188      }
189
190      /**
191       * Sets the overridden value of this bean property.
192       *
193       * @param value The overridden value of this bean property.
194       * @return This object.
195       */
196      public Builder overrideValue(Object value) {
197         overrideValue = value;
198         return this;
199      }
200
201      /**
202       * Sets the raw metadata type for this bean property.
203       *
204       * @param value The raw metadata type for this bean property.
205       * @return This object.
206       */
207      public Builder rawMetaType(BeanInfo<?> value) {
208         rawTypeMeta = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
209         typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
210         return this;
211      }
212
213      /**
214       * Sets the raw metadata type for this bean property from a {@link Class}.
215       *
216       * <p>
217       * Convenience overload that resolves the supplied class via the property's
218       * {@link BeanTypeResolver}, allowing callers from the bean-modeling layer to seed the type without
219       * holding a {@link BeanInfo} reference.
220       *
221       * <p>
222       * When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via the commons-side path
223       * ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}), no resolver is available, so
224       * {@code rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta} are left <jk>null</jk> and the property runs in raw-reflection mode.
225       *
226       * @param value The raw metadata type for this bean property.
227       * @return This object.
228       */
229      public Builder rawMetaType(Class<?> value) {
230         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
231         if (bc == null)
232            return this;
233         rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(null, info(value), null);
234         typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
235         return this;
236      }
237
238      /**
239       * Marks this property as readable.
240       *
241       * @return This object.
242       */
243      public Builder canRead() {
244         canRead = true;
245         return this;
246      }
247
248      /**
249       * Marks this property as writable.
250       *
251       * @return This object.
252       */
253      public Builder canWrite() {
254         canWrite = true;
255         return this;
256      }
257
258      /**
259       * Marks this property as a constructor argument.
260       *
261       * @return This object.
262       */
263      public Builder setAsConstructorArg() {
264         isConstructorArg = true;
265         return this;
266      }
267
268      /**
269       * Sets the extra keys method for this bean property.
270       *
271       * @param value The method info that returns extra keys for this property.
272       * @return This object.
273       */
274      public Builder setExtraKeys(MethodInfo value) {
275         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
276         extraKeys = value.accessible();
277         return this;
278      }
279
280      /**
281       * Sets the field for this bean property.
282       *
283       * @param value The field info for this bean property.
284       * @return This object.
285       */
286      public Builder setField(FieldInfo value) {
287         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
288         field = value.accessible();
289         innerField = field;
290         return this;
291      }
292
293      /**
294       * Sets the getter method for this bean property.
295       *
296       * @param value The getter method info for this bean property.
297       * @return This object.
298       */
299      public Builder setGetter(MethodInfo value) {
300         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
301         getter = value.accessible();
302         return this;
303      }
304
305      /**
306       * Sets the inner field for this bean property from a {@link FieldInfo}.
307       *
308       * @param value The field info containing the inner field.
309       * @return This object.
310       */
311      public Builder setInnerField(FieldInfo value) {
312         innerField = assertArgNotNull(ARG_innerField, value);
313         return this;
314      }
315
316      /**
317       * Sets the setter method for this bean property.
318       *
319       * @param value The setter method info for this bean property.
320       * @return This object.
321       */
322      public Builder setSetter(MethodInfo value) {
323         assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
324         setter = value.accessible();
325         return this;
326      }
327
328      /**
329       * Validates this bean property configuration.
330       *
331       * <p>
332       * After validation succeeds, marshalling-side callers (currently {@link BeanMeta}) read the
333       * {@link #dictionaryClasses} field to construct the property-level registry lookup and store it in a
334       * side-map keyed by the built {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.  The bean-modeling layer itself no longer carries a
335       * concrete registry reference.
336       *
337       * <p>
338       * When {@code bc} is <jk>null</jk> (commons-side path), this method runs in raw-reflection mode:
339       * annotation reads are routed through {@link BeanConfigContext#getAnnotationProvider()} but no
340       * {@code ClassMeta} resolution or {@code ObjectSwap} discovery is performed; the property's
341       * {@code rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta} stay <jk>null</jk> and the resulting {@link BeanPropertyMeta}
342       * exposes raw getter/setter invocation only.
343       *
344       * @param bc The bean context.  May be <jk>null</jk> for the commons-side path.
345       * @param typeVarImpls Type variable implementations.  Ignored when {@code bc} is <jk>null</jk>.
346       * @param bpro Bean properties read-only set.
347       * @param bpwo Bean properties write-only set.
348       * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is valid, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
349       * @throws Exception If validation fails.
350       */
351      @SuppressWarnings({
352         "java:S3776", // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
353         "java:S112",  // Generic exception thrown; acceptable for framework/lifecycle methods
354         "java:S6541"  // Brain Method: validate() intentionally consolidates property metadata resolution
355      })
356      public boolean validate(BeanTypeResolver bc, TypeVariables typeVarImpls, Set<String> bpro, Set<String> bpwo) throws Exception {
357
358         var ap = nn(bc) ? bc.getAnnotationProvider() : config.getAnnotationProvider();
359
360         if (field == null && getter == null && setter == null)
361            return false;
362
363         // Settings reads route through BeanConfigContext so the commons-side path works without a MarshallingContext.
364         if (field == null && setter == null && config.isBeansRequireSettersForGetters() && ! isConstructorArg)
365            return false;
366
367         canRead |= (nn(field) || nn(getter));
368         canWrite |= (nn(field) || nn(setter) || isConstructorArg);
369
370         var ifi = innerField;
371         var gi = getter;
372         var si = setter;
373
374         // @MarshalledProp / @Swap annotation reads have been lifted out to the marshalling-side post-processor
375         // (see {@link org.apache.juneau.MarshalledPropertyPostProcessor#process}).  They are processed by {@link BeanMeta}
376         // after this method returns.  The post-processor mutates {@link #swap}, {@link #properties},
377         // {@link #dictionaryClasses}, and {@link #typeMeta} (when a swap is detected) on this builder.
378         dictionaryClasses = liste();
379
380         if (nn(innerField)) {
381            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, ifi);
382            if (nn(bc) && (nn(field) || ne(lbp))) {
383               // Only use field type if it's a bean property or has @BeanProp annotation.
384               // Otherwise, we want to infer the type from the getter or setter.
385               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(opt(last(lbp)).orElse(null), innerField.getFieldType(), typeVarImpls);
386               isUri |= (rawTypeMeta.isUri());
387            }
388            lbp.forEach(x -> {
389               var beanp = x.inner();
390               if (ne(beanp.ro()))
391                  readOnly = bool(beanp.ro());
392               if (ne(beanp.wo()))
393                  writeOnly = bool(beanp.wo());
394            });
395         }
396
397         if (nn(getter)) {
398            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, gi);
399            if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
400               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(opt(last(lbp)).orElse(null), getter.getReturnType(), typeVarImpls);
401            if (nn(rawTypeMeta))
402               isUri |= rawTypeMeta.isUri();
403            lbp.forEach(x -> {
404               var beanp = x.inner();
405               if (ne(beanp.ro()))
406                  readOnly = bool(beanp.ro());
407               if (ne(beanp.wo()))
408                  writeOnly = bool(beanp.wo());
409            });
410         }
411
412         if (nn(setter)) {
413            var lbp = ap.find(BeanProp.class, si);
414            if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
415               rawTypeMeta = bc.resolveType(opt(last(lbp)).orElse(null), setter.getParameterTypes().get(0), typeVarImpls);
416            if (nn(rawTypeMeta))
417               isUri |= rawTypeMeta.isUri();
418            lbp.forEach(x -> {
419               var beanp = x.inner();
420               if (ne(beanp.ro()))
421                  readOnly = bool(beanp.ro());
422               if (ne(beanp.wo()))
423                  writeOnly = bool(beanp.wo());
424            });
425         }
426
427         // On the commons-side path (bc == null), rawTypeMeta stays null and validate() accepts the property
428         // in raw-reflection mode.  The marshalling-side path still requires a resolvable type.
429         if (nn(bc) && rawTypeMeta == null)
430            return false;
431
432         isDyna = "*".equals(name);
433
434         // Do some annotation validation.  Type-aware validation requires rawTypeMeta — on the commons-side
435         // path we skip it entirely.
436         if (nn(rawTypeMeta)) {
437            var ci = rawTypeMeta;
438            if (nn(getter)) {
439               var pt = getter.getParameterTypes();
440               if (isDyna) {
441                  if (ci.isAssignableTo(Map.class) && e(pt)) {
442                     isDynaGetterMap = true;
443                  } else if (pt.size() == 1 && pt.get(0).is(String.class)) {
444                     // OK.
445                  } else {
446                     return false;
447                  }
448               } else {
449                  if (! ci.isAssignableTo(getter.getReturnType()))
450                     return false;
451               }
452            }
453            if (nn(setter)) {
454               var pt = setter.getParameterTypes();
455               if (isDyna) {
456                  if (pt.size() == 2 && pt.get(0).is(String.class)) {
457                     // OK.
458                  } else {
459                     return false;
460                  }
461               } else {
462                  if (pt.size() != 1 || ! ci.isAssignableTo(pt.get(0).inner()))
463                     return false;
464               }
465            }
466            if (nn(field)) {
467               if (isDyna) {
468                  if (! field.getFieldType().isAssignableTo(Map.class))
469                     return false;
470               } else {
471                  if (! ci.isAssignableTo(field.getFieldType()))
472                     return false;
473               }
474            }
475
476            if (isDyna) {
477               rawTypeMeta = rawTypeMeta.getValueType();
478               if (rawTypeMeta == null && nn(bc))
479                  rawTypeMeta = bc.objectType();
480            }
481
482            if (typeMeta == null) {
483               if (rawTypeMeta == null && nn(bc))
484                  typeMeta = bc.objectType();
485               else
486                  typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
487            }
488            if (typeMeta == null)
489               typeMeta = rawTypeMeta;
490         }
491
492         if (bpro.contains(name) || bpro.contains("*"))
493            readOnly = true;
494         if (bpwo.contains(name) || bpwo.contains("*"))
495            writeOnly = true;
496
497         return true;
498      }
499
500   }
501
502   /**
503    * Creates a builder for {@link #BeanPropertyMeta} objects.
504    *
505    * @param beanMeta The metadata on the bean
506    * @param name The bean property name.
507    * @return A new builder.
508    */
509   public static Builder builder(BeanMeta<?> beanMeta, String name) {
510      return new Builder(beanMeta, name);
511   }
512
513   private final AnnotationProvider ap;                             // Annotation provider for finding annotations on this property.  Sourced from bc (marshalling-side) or beanMeta.getConfig() (commons-side).
514   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<?>>> annotations;     // Memoized list of all annotations on this property.
515   private final BeanTypeResolver bc;                               // The bean-modeling SPI seam to the marshalling-side type resolver.  Null when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path.
516   private final BeanConfigContext config;                          // Bean-modeling settings facade — always non-null.  Mirrors the BeanMeta's config.
517   private final BeanMeta<?> beanMeta;                              // The bean that this property belongs to.
518   private final boolean canRead;                                   // True if this property can be read.
519   private final boolean canWrite;                                  // True if this property can be written.
520   private final BeanPropertyMeta delegateFor;                      // The bean property that this meta is a delegate for.
521   private final MethodInfo extraKeys;                              // The bean property extraKeys method.
522   private final FieldInfo field;                                   // The bean property field (if it has one).
523   private final MethodInfo getter;                                 // The bean property getter.
524   private final int hashCode;                                      // Cached hash code for this property meta.
525   private final FieldInfo innerField;                              // The bean property field even if private (if it has one).
526   private final boolean isDyna;                                    // True if this is a dyna property (i.e. name="*").
527   private final boolean isDynaGetterMap;                           // True if this is a dyna property where the getter returns a Map directly.
528   private final boolean isUri;                                     // True if this is a URL/URI or annotated with @URI.
529   private final String name;                                       // The name of the property.
530   private final Object overrideValue;                              // The bean property value (if it's an overridden delegate).
531   private final BeanInfo<?> rawTypeMeta;                           // The real class type of the bean property.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam for the eventual move to commons.bean.
532   private final BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> readTransform;  // Applied to raw getter result; identity by default.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
533   private final boolean readOnly;                                  // True if this property is read-only.
534   private final MethodInfo setter;                                 // The bean property setter.
535   private final BeanInfo<?> typeMeta;                              // The transformed class type of the bean property.  Concrete instances are always {@code ClassMeta}; typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
536   private final BiFunction<BeanSession,Object,Object> writeTransform; // Applied to incoming value before raw setter; identity by default.  Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
537   private final boolean writeOnly;                                 // True if this property is write-only.
538   private final Set<String> views;                                 // Named views this property belongs to; null = untagged (included in all views when defaultViewInclusion is enabled).
539
540   /**
541    * Creates a new BeanPropertyMeta using the contents of the specified builder.
542    *
543    * @param b The builder to copy fields from.
544    */
545   protected BeanPropertyMeta(Builder b) {
546      annotations = memoize(this::findAnnotations);
547      bc = b.bc;
548      config = b.config;
549      beanMeta = b.beanMeta;
550      canRead = b.canRead;
551      canWrite = b.canWrite;
552      delegateFor = b.delegateFor;
553      extraKeys = b.extraKeys;
554      field = b.field;
555      getter = b.getter;
556      innerField = b.innerField;
557      isDyna = b.isDyna;
558      isDynaGetterMap = b.isDynaGetterMap;
559      isUri = b.isUri;
560      name = b.name;
561      overrideValue = b.overrideValue;
562      rawTypeMeta = b.rawTypeMeta;
563      readOnly = b.readOnly;
564      setter = b.setter;
565      typeMeta = b.typeMeta;
566      writeOnly = b.writeOnly;
567      views = b.views != null ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(b.views) : null;
568      readTransform = b.readTransform != null ? b.readTransform : (session, o) -> o;
569      writeTransform = b.writeTransform != null ? b.writeTransform : (session, o) -> o;
570
571      ap = nn(bc) ? bc.getAnnotationProvider() : b.config.getAnnotationProvider();
572      hashCode = h(beanMeta, name);
573   }
574
575   /**
576    * Adds a value to a {@link Collection} or array property.
577    *
578    * <p>
579    * Note that adding values to an array property is inefficient for large arrays since it must copy the array into a
580    * larger array on each operation.
581    *
582    * <p>
583    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
584    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta} and a non-null backing
585    * {@code MarshallingSession} on the supplied {@link BeanMap}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
586    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
587    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because adding into a Collection/array property requires
588    * type-aware element conversion (the marshalling session's {@code convertToType}) that is not available in the
589    * bean-modeling-only path.
590    *
591    * @param m The bean of the field being set.
592    * @param pName
593    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
594    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
595    * @param value The value to add to the field.
596    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If field is not a collection or array.
597    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
598    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
599    */
600   @SuppressWarnings({
601      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for property add operation with various collection types
602   })
603   public void add(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
604
605      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
606         throw unsupportedOp("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; Collection/array add operations require a marshalling context.", name);
607
608      // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
609      if (m.bean == null) {
610         if (! m.propertyCache.containsKey(name))
611            m.propertyCache.put(name, new ArrayList<>());
612         ((List)m.propertyCache.get(name)).add(value);
613         return;
614      }
615
616      var session = m.getBeanSession();
617
618      var isCollection = rawTypeMeta.isCollection();
619      var isArray = rawTypeMeta.isArray();
620
621      if (! (isCollection || isArray))
622         throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Attempt to add element to property ''{0}'' which is not a collection or array", name);
623
624      var bean = m.getBean(true);
625
626      var elementType = rawTypeMeta.getElementType();
627
628      try {
629         var v = session.convertToType(value, elementType);
630
631         if (isCollection) {
632            Collection c = (Collection)invokeGetter(bean, pName);
633
634            Collection c2 = null;
635            if (nn(c)) {
636               if (canAddTo(c)) {
637                  c.add(v);
638                  return;
639               }
640               c2 = c;
641            }
642
643            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance())
644               c = (Collection)rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
645            else
646               c = new ArrayList<>();
647
648            if (c2 != null)
649               c.addAll(c2);
650
651            c.add(v);
652
653            invokeSetter(bean, pName, c);
654
655         } else /* isArray() */ {
656
657            if (m.arrayPropertyCache == null)
658               m.arrayPropertyCache = new TreeMap<>();
659
660            List l = m.arrayPropertyCache.get(name);
661            if (l == null) {
662               l = new LinkedList();  // ArrayLists and LinkLists appear to perform equally.
663               m.arrayPropertyCache.put(name, l);
664
665               // Copy any existing array values into the temporary list.
666               var oldArray = invokeGetter(bean, pName);
667               copyArrayToList(oldArray, l);
668            }
669
670            // Add new entry to our array.
671            l.add(v);
672         }
673
674      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
675         throw e;
676      } catch (Exception e) {
677         throw bex(e);
678      }
679   }
680
681   /**
682    * Adds a value to a {@link Map} or bean property.
683    *
684    * <p>
685    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
686    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta} and a non-null backing
687    * {@code MarshallingSession} on the supplied {@link BeanMap}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
688    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
689    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because adding into a Map/bean property requires type-aware
690    * value conversion (the marshalling session's {@code convertToType} / {@code toBeanMap}) that is not available
691    * in the bean-modeling-only path.
692    *
693    * @param m The bean of the field being set.
694    * @param pName
695    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
696    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
697    * @param key The key to add to the field.
698    * @param value The value to add to the field.
699    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If field is not a map or array.
700    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
701    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
702    */
703   @SuppressWarnings({
704      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for property add operation with map key handling
705   })
706   public void add(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, String key, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
707
708      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
709         throw unsupportedOp("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; Map/bean add operations require a marshalling context.", name);
710
711      // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
712      if (m.bean == null) {
713         if (! m.propertyCache.containsKey(name))
714            m.propertyCache.put(name, new LinkedHashMap<>());
715         ((Map)m.propertyCache.get(name)).put(key, value);
716         return;
717      }
718
719      var session = m.getBeanSession();
720
721      var isMap = rawTypeMeta.isMap();
722      var isBean = rawTypeMeta.isBean();
723
724      if (! (isBean || isMap))
725         throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Attempt to add key/value to property ''{0}'' which is not a map or bean", name);
726
727      var bean = m.getBean(true);
728
729      try {
730         var v = session.convertToType(value, rawTypeMeta.getElementType());
731
732         if (isMap) {
733            var map = (Map)invokeGetter(bean, pName);
734
735            if (nn(map)) {
736               map.put(key, v);
737               return;
738            }
739
740            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance())
741               map = (Map)rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
742            else
743               map = new LinkedHashMap<>();
744
745            map.put(key, v);
746
747            invokeSetter(bean, pName, map);
748
749         } else /* isBean() */ {
750
751            var b = invokeGetter(bean, pName);
752
753            if (nn(b)) {
754               ((Map) session.toBeanMap(b)).put(key, v);
755               return;
756            }
757
758            if (rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance(m.getBean(false))) {
759               b = rawTypeMeta.newInstance();
760               ((Map) session.toBeanMap(b)).put(key, v);
761            }
762
763            invokeSetter(bean, pName, b);
764         }
765
766      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
767         throw e;
768      } catch (Exception e) {
769         throw bex(e);
770      }
771   }
772
773   /**
774    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property can be read.
775    *
776    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property can be read.
777    */
778   public boolean canRead() {
779      return canRead;
780   }
781
782   /**
783    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property can be written.
784    *
785    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property can be written.
786    */
787   public boolean canWrite() {
788      return canWrite;
789   }
790
791   @Override /* Overridden from Comparable */
792   public int compareTo(BeanPropertyMeta o) {
793      return cmp(name, o.name);
794   }
795
796   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
797   public boolean equals(Object o) {
798      return (o instanceof BeanPropertyMeta o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.name, y.name) && eq(x.beanMeta, y.beanMeta));
799   }
800
801   /**
802    * Returns all annotations on this property (field, getter, and setter).
803    *
804    * <p>
805    * The annotations are found on:
806    * <ul>
807    *    <li>The field (if present)
808    *    <li>The getter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
809    *    <li>The setter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
810    * </ul>
811    *
812    * <p>
813    * The result is memoized and computed only once.
814    *
815    * @return A list of all annotation infos on this property. Never <jk>null</jk>.
816    */
817   public List<AnnotationInfo> getAnnotations() {
818      return (List)annotations.get();
819   }
820
821   /**
822    * Returns a stream of annotations of the specified type on this property.
823    *
824    * <p>
825    * The annotations are found on:
826    * <ul>
827    *    <li>The field (if present)
828    *    <li>The getter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
829    *    <li>The setter method (if present) - including annotations on the method, its return type, and package
830    * </ul>
831    *
832    * @param <A> The annotation type to find.
833    * @param a The annotation class to find.
834    * @return A stream of annotation infos of the specified type. Never <jk>null</jk>.
835    */
836   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> a) {
837      if (a == null)
838         return Stream.empty();
839      return annotations.get().stream()
840         .filter(x -> x.isType(a))
841         .map(x -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)x);
842   }
843
844   /**
845    * Helper method to find all annotations on this property.
846    *
847    * @return A list of all annotation infos found on the field, getter, and setter.
848    */
849   private List<AnnotationInfo<?>> findAnnotations() {
850      var result = new ArrayList<AnnotationInfo<?>>();
851      if (nn(field))
852         result.addAll(ap.find(field));
853      if (nn(getter))
854         result.addAll(ap.find(getter, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
855      if (nn(setter))
856         result.addAll(ap.find(setter, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
857      if (nn(extraKeys))
858         result.addAll(ap.find(extraKeys, SELF, MATCHING_METHODS, RETURN_TYPE, PACKAGE));
859      return u(result);
860   }
861
862   /**
863    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#get(Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property meta.
864    *
865    * @param m The bean map to get the transformed value from.
866    * @param pName
867    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
868    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
869    * @return
870    *    The property value.
871    *    <br>Returns <jk>null</jk> if this is a write-only property.
872    */
873   public Object get(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
874      return m.meta.onReadProperty(m.bean, pName, getInner(m, pName));
875   }
876
877   /**
878    * Returns the bean meta that this property belongs to.
879    *
880    * @return The bean meta that this property belongs to.
881    */
882   public BeanMeta<?> getBeanMeta() { return beanMeta; }
883
884   /**
885    * Returns the bean dictionary in use for this bean property.
886    *
887    * <p>
888    * The order of lookup for the dictionary is as follows:
889    * <ol>
890    *    <li>Dictionary defined via {@code @MarshalledProp(dictionary)}.
891    *    <li>Dictionary defined via {@code MarshallingContext.Builder<?>#beanDictionary(Class...)}.
892    * </ol>
893    *
894    * <p>
895    * The per-property registry no longer lives on this object; it is stored in a marshalling-side
896    * side-map on {@link BeanMeta} keyed by {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.  This method delegates to
897    * {@link BeanMeta#getPropertyBeanRegistry(BeanPropertyMeta)} for backwards compatibility with existing call sites
898    * in the marshalling layer (parser/serializer sessions, XML content-property handling, etc.).
899    *
900    * <p>
901    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanRegistryLookup}).  Marshalling-side callers that need
902    * implementation-specific behavior cast at the call site.
903    *
904    * @return The bean dictionary in use for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if no registry was registered.
905    */
906   public BeanRegistryLookup getBeanRegistry() { return beanMeta.getPropertyBeanRegistry(this); }
907
908   /**
909    * Returns the {@link BeanInfo} of the class of this property.
910    *
911    * <p>
912    * If this property or the property type class has a {@code ObjectSwap} associated with it, this method returns the
913    * transformed class meta.
914    * This matches the class type that is used by the {@link #get(BeanMap,String)} and
915    * {@link #set(BeanMap,String,Object)} methods.
916    *
917    * <p>
918    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanInfo}).  Marshalling-side callers that need the
919    * {@code ClassMeta} narrowing must cast — the concrete instance in-tree is always a {@code ClassMeta}.
920    *
921    * @return The {@link BeanInfo} of the class of this property, or <jk>null</jk> if this property was built via
922    *    the bean-modeling-only path.
923    */
924   public BeanInfo<?> getBeanInfo() { return typeMeta; }
925
926   /**
927    * Returns the metadata on the property that this metadata is a delegate for.
928    *
929    * @return the metadata on the property that this metadata is a delegate for, or this object if it's not a delegate.
930    */
931   public BeanPropertyMeta getDelegateFor() { return def(delegateFor, this); }
932
933   /**
934    * Returns the {@link Map} object returned by the DynaBean getter.
935    *
936    * <p>
937    * The DynaBean property is the property whose name is <js>"*"</js> and returns a map of "extra" properties on the
938    * bean.
939    *
940    * @param bean The bean.
941    * @return
942    *    The map returned by the getter, or an empty map if the getter returned <jk>null</jk> or this isn't a DynaBean
943    *    property.
944    * @throws IllegalArgumentException Thrown by method invocation.
945    * @throws IllegalAccessException Thrown by method invocation.
946    * @throws InvocationTargetException Thrown by method invocation.
947    */
948   public Map<String,Object> getDynaMap(Object bean) throws IllegalArgumentException, IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException {
949      if (isDyna) {
950         if (nn(extraKeys) && nn(getter) && ! isDynaGetterMap) {
951            Map<String,Object> m = map();
952            ((Collection<String>)extraKeys.invoke(bean)).forEach(x -> safe(() -> m.put(x, getter.invoke(bean, x))));
953            return m;
954         }
955         if (nn(getter) && isDynaGetterMap)
956            return (Map)getter.invoke(bean);
957         if (nn(field))
958            return (Map)field.get(bean);
959         throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
960      }
961      return mape();
962   }
963
964   /**
965    * Returns the field for this property.
966    *
967    * @return The field info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no field associated with this bean property.
968    */
969   public FieldInfo getField() { return field; }
970
971   /**
972    * Returns the getter method for this property.
973    *
974    * @return The getter method info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no getter method.
975    */
976   public MethodInfo getGetter() { return getter; }
977
978   /**
979    * Returns the field for this property even if the field is private.
980    *
981    * @return The field info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no field associated with this bean property.
982    */
983   public FieldInfo getInnerField() { return innerField; }
984
985   /**
986    * Returns the name of this bean property.
987    *
988    * @return The name of the bean property.
989    */
990   public String getName() { return name; }
991
992   /**
993    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#getRaw(Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property meta.
994    *
995    * @param m The bean map to get the transformed value from.
996    * @param pName
997    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
998    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
999    * @return The raw property value.
1000    */
1001   public Object getRaw(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
1002      try {
1003         // Read-only beans have their properties stored in a cache until getBean() is called.
1004         var bean = m.bean;
1005         if (bean == null)
1006            return m.propertyCache.get(name);
1007
1008      return invokeGetter(bean, pName);
1009
1010   } catch (Exception e) {
1011      if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()) {
1012         if (nn(rawTypeMeta) && rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1013            return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1014         return null;
1015      }
1016      throw bex(e, beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Exception occurred while getting property ''{0}''", name);
1017   }
1018   }
1019
1020   /**
1021    * Returns the setter method for this property.
1022    *
1023    * @return The setter method info for this bean property, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no setter method.
1024    */
1025   public MethodInfo getSetter() { return setter; }
1026
1027   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1028   public int hashCode() {
1029      return hashCode;
1030   }
1031
1032   /**
1033    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is named <js>"*"</js>.
1034    *
1035    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is named <js>"*"</js>.
1036    */
1037   public boolean isDyna() { return isDyna; }
1038
1039   /**
1040    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property is read-only.
1041    *
1042    * <p>
1043    * This implies the property MIGHT be writable, but that parsers should not set a value for it.
1044    *
1045    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is read-only.
1046    */
1047   public boolean isReadOnly() { return readOnly; }
1048
1049   /**
1050    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is a URI.
1051    *
1052    * <p>
1053    * A bean property can be considered a URI if any of the following are true:
1054    * <ul>
1055    *    <li>Property class type is {@link URL} or {@link URI}.
1056    *    <li>Property class type is annotated with {@code @Uri}.
1057    *    <li>Property getter, setter, or field is annotated with {@code @Uri}.
1058    * </ul>
1059    *
1060    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this bean property is a URI.
1061    */
1062   public boolean isUri() { return isUri; }
1063
1064   /**
1065    * Returns the set of named views this property belongs to, or <jk>null</jk> if the property is untagged.
1066    *
1067    * <p>
1068    * An untagged property (one with no {@code @MarshalledProp(view=...)} annotation) follows the
1069    * default-view-inclusion policy: it is included under every active view when the policy is enabled
1070    * (the default behavior), or excluded from all views when the policy is disabled.
1071    *
1072    * <p>
1073    * A tagged property is included only when the active view name is contained in this set.
1074    *
1075    * @return The set of named views, or <jk>null</jk> if no view membership was declared.
1076    */
1077   public Set<String> getViews() { return views; }
1078
1079   /**
1080    * Equivalent to calling {@link BeanMap#put(String, Object)}, but is faster since it avoids looking up the property
1081    * meta.
1082    *
1083    * <p>
1084    * This is a no-op on a read-only property.
1085    *
1086    * @param m The bean map to set the property value on.
1087    * @param pName
1088    *    The property name if this is a dyna property (i.e. <js>"*"</js>).
1089    *    <br>Otherwise can be <jk>null</jk>.
1090    * @param value The value to set.
1091    * @return The previous property value.
1092    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If property could not be set.
1093    */
1094   @SuppressWarnings({
1095      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
1096      "java:S6541", // Session objects are single-threaded by design
1097   })
1098   public Object set(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value) throws BeanRuntimeException {
1099      Object value1 = m.meta.onWriteProperty(m.bean, pName, value);
1100      try {
1101
1102         if (readOnly)
1103            return null;
1104
1105         var session = m.getBeanSession();
1106
1107         // Apply the install-time write transform (identity by default; swap-aware in the marshalling layer).
1108         value1 = writeTransform.apply(session, value1);
1109
1110         // Generic SPI hook: a transform that returns SKIP_VALUE causes the set to be skipped entirely so the
1111         // bean's pre-existing value (typically a field-initializer) is preserved.  Used by the marshalling
1112         // layer's per-property null-coercion SKIP mode.
1113         if (value1 == SKIP_VALUE)
1114            return null;
1115
1116         if (m.bean == null) {
1117
1118            // Read-only beans get their properties stored in a cache.
1119            if (nn(m.propertyCache))
1120               return m.propertyCache.put(name, value1);
1121
1122            throw bex("Non-existent bean instance on bean.");
1123         }
1124
1125         // Raw-reflection path: when the owning BeanMeta was built via the commons-side path, rawTypeMeta and
1126         // bc are null and there is no marshalling-aware type conversion to perform.  Just invoke the setter
1127         // (or write the field) directly.
1128         if (rawTypeMeta == null) {
1129            var bean = m.getBean(true);
1130            var old = (nn(getter) || nn(field)) ? get(m, pName) : null;
1131            invokeSetter(bean, pName, value1);
1132            return old;
1133         }
1134
1135         var isMap = rawTypeMeta.isMap();
1136         var isCollection = rawTypeMeta.isCollection();
1137
1138         if ((! isDyna) && field == null && setter == null && ! (isMap || isCollection)) {
1139            if ((value1 == null && config.isIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()) || config.isIgnoreMissingSetters())
1140               return null;
1141            throw bex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Setter or public field not defined on property ''{0}''", name);
1142         }
1143
1144         var bean = m.getBean(true);  // Don't use getBean() because it triggers array creation!
1145
1146         return setPropertyValue(m, pName, value1, bean, isMap, isCollection, session);
1147      } catch (org.apache.juneau.commons.BasicRuntimeException e2) {
1148         // Preserve previous behavior: marshalling-side BasicRuntimeException (notably ParseException raised by
1149         // session.parseToMap/parseToList or writeTransform) gets wrapped in BeanRuntimeException so callers can
1150         // uniformly catch BeanRuntimeException.  BeanRuntimeException itself does not extend BasicRuntimeException
1151         // so it propagates unchanged.
1152         throw bex(e2);
1153      }
1154   }
1155
1156   @SuppressWarnings({
1157      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for complex property setter
1158      "java:S6541" // Brain method acceptable - complex property value setting logic requires high LOC/complexity
1159   })
1160   private Object setPropertyValue(BeanMap<?> m, String pName, Object value1, Object bean, boolean isMap, boolean isCollection, BeanSession session) {
1161      try {
1162         var r = (config.isBeanMapPutReturnsOldValue() || isMap || isCollection) && (nn(getter) || nn(field)) ? get(m, pName) : null;
1163         var propertyClass = rawTypeMeta.inner();
1164         var pcInfo = rawTypeMeta;
1165
1166         if (value1 == null && (isMap || isCollection)) {
1167            invokeSetter(bean, pName, null);
1168            return r;
1169         }
1170
1171         var vc = value1 == null ? null : value1.getClass();
1172
1173         if (isMap && (setter == null || ! pcInfo.isAssignableFrom(vc))) {
1174
1175            if (! (value1 instanceof Map)) {
1176               if (value1 instanceof CharSequence value21)
1177                  value1 = session.parseToMap(value21);
1178               else
1179                  throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}''", name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1180            }
1181
1182            var valueMap = (Map)value1;
1183            var propMap = (Map)r;
1184            var keyType = rawTypeMeta.getKeyType();
1185            var valueType = rawTypeMeta.getValueType();
1186
1187            // If the property type is abstract, then we either need to reuse the existing
1188            // map (if it's not null), or try to assign the value directly.
1189            if (! rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance()) {
1190               if (propMap == null) {
1191                  if (setter == null && field == null)
1192                     throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1193                        "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter or public field is defined, and the current value is null", name,
1194                        propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1195
1196                  if (propertyClass.isInstance(valueMap)) {
1197                     if (! (keyType.isObject() && valueType.isObject())) {
1198                        var needsConversion = Flag.create();
1199                        valueMap.forEach((k, v2) -> {
1200                           if (nn(k) && ! keyType.isObject() && ! keyType.isInstance(k))
1201                              needsConversion.set();
1202                           if (nn(v2) && ! valueType.isInstance(v2)) {
1203                              needsConversion.set();
1204                           }
1205                        });
1206                        if (needsConversion.isSet())
1207                           valueMap = (Map)session.convertToType(valueMap, rawTypeMeta);
1208                     }
1209                     invokeSetter(bean, pName, valueMap);
1210                     return r;
1211                  }
1212                  throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1213                     "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{2}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because the assigned map cannot be converted to the specified type because the property type is abstract, and the property value is currently null",
1214                     name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1215               }
1216            } else {
1217               if (propMap == null) {
1218                  propMap = BeanInstantiator.of(Map.class).type(rawTypeMeta).preferZeroArgConstructor().run();
1219               } else {
1220                  propMap.clear();
1221               }
1222            }
1223
1224            // Set the values.
1225            for (var e : ((Set<Map.Entry>)valueMap.entrySet())) {
1226               var k1 = e.getKey();
1227               var v1 = e.getValue();
1228               if (! keyType.isObject())
1229                  k1 = session.convertToType(k1, keyType);
1230               if (! valueType.isObject())
1231                  v1 = session.convertToType(v1, valueType);
1232               propMap.put(k1, v1);
1233            }
1234            if (nn(setter) || nn(field))
1235               invokeSetter(bean, pName, propMap);
1236
1237         } else if (isCollection && (setter == null || ! pcInfo.isAssignableFrom(vc))) {
1238
1239            if (! (value1 instanceof Collection)) {
1240               if (value1 instanceof CharSequence value2)
1241                  value1 = session.parseToList(value2);
1242               else
1243                  throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}''", name, propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1244            }
1245
1246            var valueList = (Collection)value1;
1247            var propList = (Collection)r;
1248            var elementType = rawTypeMeta.getElementType();
1249
1250            // If the property type is abstract, then we either need to reuse the existing
1251            // collection (if it's not null), or try to assign the value directly.
1252            if (! rawTypeMeta.canCreateNewInstance()) {
1253               if (propList == null) {
1254                  if (setter == null && field == null)
1255                     throw bex(beanMeta.getBeanInfo(),
1256                        "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter or public field is defined, and the current value is null", name,
1257                        propertyClass.getName(), cn(value1));
1258
1259                  if (propertyClass.isInstance(valueList) || (nn(setter) && setter.getParameterTypes().get(0).is(Collection.class))) {
1260                     if (! elementType.isObject()) {
1261                        // Element coercion is required.  Build the coerced elements into a concrete collection
1262                        // whose shape is assignable to the (abstract) property type — never a bare ArrayList —
1263                        // so Deque/Queue/SortedSet fields aren't handed an incompatible container.  (The source
1264                        // value can incidentally satisfy propertyClass.isInstance(...) — e.g. the parser's
1265                        // generic list is a LinkedList, which is-a Deque/Queue — yet still need coercion.)
1266                        Collection coerced = createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(propertyClass);
1267                        for (var v : valueList) {
1268                           var needsConversion = v == null ? elementType.isOptional() : ! elementType.isInstance(v);
1269                           coerced.add(needsConversion ? session.convertToType(v, elementType) : v);
1270                        }
1271                        valueList = coerced;
1272                     }
1273                     invokeSetter(bean, pName, valueList);
1274                     return r;
1275                  }
1276
1277                  // Robust best-effort-then-fallback materialization — never returns null (see helper Javadoc).
1278                  propList = createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(propertyClass);
1279                  invokeSetter(bean, pName, propList);
1280               }
1281               propList.clear();
1282            } else {
1283               if (propList == null) {
1284                  propList = BeanInstantiator.of(Collection.class).type(rawTypeMeta).preferZeroArgConstructor().run();
1285                  invokeSetter(bean, pName, propList);
1286               } else {
1287                  propList.clear();
1288               }
1289            }
1290
1291            // Set the values.
1292            for (var x : valueList) {
1293               if (! elementType.isObject())
1294                  x = session.convertToType(x, elementType);
1295               propList.add(x);
1296            }
1297
1298         } else {
1299            value1 = session.convertToMemberType(bean, value1, rawTypeMeta);
1300            invokeSetter(bean, pName, value1);
1301         }
1302
1303         return r;
1304
1305      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
1306         throw e;
1307      } catch (Exception e1) {
1308         if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnSetters()) {
1309            if (rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1310               return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1311            return null;
1312         }
1313         throw bex(e1, beanMeta.getBeanInfo(), "Error occurred trying to set property ''{0}''", name);
1314      }
1315   }
1316
1317   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1318      // @formatter:off
1319      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
1320         .a(PROP_field, field)
1321         .a(PROP_getter, getter)
1322         .a(PROP_name, name)
1323         .a(PROP_setter, setter)
1324         .a(PROP_type, cn(rawTypeMeta));
1325      // @formatter:on
1326   }
1327
1328   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
1329   public String toString() {
1330      return r(properties());
1331   }
1332
1333   private Object getInner(BeanMap<?> m, String pName) {
1334      try {
1335
1336         if (writeOnly)
1337            return null;
1338
1339         if (nn(overrideValue))
1340            return overrideValue;
1341
1342         // Read-only beans have their properties stored in a cache until getBean() is called.
1343         var bean = m.bean;
1344         if (bean == null)
1345            return m.propertyCache.get(name);
1346
1347         var session = m.getBeanSession();
1348         var o = getRaw(m, pName);
1349
1350         return readTransform.apply(session, o);
1351
1352      } catch (Exception e) {
1353         if (config.isIgnoreInvocationExceptionsOnGetters()) {
1354            if (nn(rawTypeMeta) && rawTypeMeta.isPrimitive())
1355               return rawTypeMeta.getPrimitiveDefault();
1356            return null;
1357         }
1358         throw bex(e, beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Exception occurred while getting property ''{0}''", name);
1359      }
1360   }
1361
1362   private Object invokeGetter(Object bean, String pName) throws IllegalArgumentException {
1363      if (isDyna) {
1364         Map m = null;
1365         if (nn(getter)) {
1366            if (! isDynaGetterMap)
1367               return getter.invoke(bean, pName);
1368            m = (Map)getter.invoke(bean);
1369         } else if (nn(field))
1370            m = (Map)field.get(bean);
1371         else
1372            throw bex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
1373         return (m == null ? null : m.get(pName));
1374      }
1375      if (nn(getter))
1376         return getter.invoke(bean);
1377      if (nn(field))
1378         return field.get(bean);
1379      throw bex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), MSG_getterOrFieldNotDefined, name);
1380   }
1381
1382   private Object invokeSetter(Object bean, String pName, Object val) throws IllegalArgumentException {
1383      if (isDyna) {
1384         if (nn(setter))
1385            return setter.invoke(bean, pName, val);
1386         Map m = null;
1387         if (nn(field))
1388            m = (Map<String,Object>)field.get(bean);
1389         else if (nn(getter))
1390            m = (Map<String,Object>)getter.invoke(bean);
1391         else
1392            throw bex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter is defined on this property, and the existing property value is null",
1393               name, classNameForError(), cn(val));
1394         return (m == null ? null : m.put(pName, val));
1395      }
1396      if (nn(setter))
1397         return setter.invoke(bean, val);
1398      if (nn(field)) {
1399         field.set(bean, val);
1400         return null;
1401      }
1402      throw bex(beanMeta.getClassInfo(), "Cannot set property ''{0}'' of type ''{1}'' to object of type ''{2}'' because no setter is defined on this property, and the existing property value is null", name,
1403         classNameForError(), cn(val));
1404   }
1405
1406   private String classNameForError() {
1407      var cm = getBeanInfo();
1408      if (nn(cm))
1409         return cm.getName();
1410      return beanMeta.getClassInfo().getName();
1411   }
1412
1413   /**
1414    * Returns a concrete {@link Collection} instance suitable for populating an abstract collection-typed bean
1415    * property that has no setter and no {@code @BeanProp(type=...)} override.
1416    *
1417    * <p>
1418    * Resolution is <b>best-effort-then-fallback</b>:
1419    * <ol>
1420    *    <li><b>Best-effort materialization of the declared type.</b>  First attempts to instantiate
1421    *       {@code propertyClass} directly via {@link BeanInstantiator} (preferring a zero-arg constructor).  This
1422    *       succeeds when the declared type is itself instantiable — e.g. a concrete {@link AbstractSet} /
1423    *       {@link AbstractList} subclass with an accessible no-arg constructor, or any type
1424    *       {@code BeanInstantiator} can otherwise construct.  The attempt never throws; on any failure it silently
1425    *       degrades to the shape-based fallback below.
1426    *    <li><b>Shape-based fallback.</b>  When the declared type is genuinely not instantiable (a {@link Set} /
1427    *       {@link List} / {@link Queue} interface, a truly abstract class, or a class with no usable constructor),
1428    *       a default concrete type is chosen by collection shape:
1429    *       <ul>
1430    *          <li>{@link SortedSet} / {@link NavigableSet} &rarr; {@link TreeSet}
1431    *          <li>{@link Set} &rarr; {@link LinkedHashSet} (preserves insertion order)
1432    *          <li>{@link Deque} / {@link Queue} &rarr; {@link ArrayDeque}
1433    *          <li>{@link List} / raw {@link Collection} (and any other collection shape) &rarr; {@link ArrayList}
1434    *       </ul>
1435    * </ol>
1436    *
1437    * @param propertyClass The declared (abstract or interface) collection type of the property.
1438    * @return A new, empty, concrete {@link Collection} instance.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1439    */
1440   private Collection<?> createDefaultCollectionForAbstractType(Class<?> propertyClass) {
1441      // Best-effort: try to materialize the declared type itself (handles concrete AbstractSet/AbstractList
1442      // subclasses, registered defaults, etc.).  Never throws — degrades to the shape-based fallback on failure.
1443      Collection declared = BeanInstantiator.of(Collection.class).type(info(propertyClass)).preferZeroArgConstructor().fallback(() -> null).run();
1444      if (nn(declared))
1445         return declared;
1446
1447      // Shape-based fallback for genuinely non-instantiable declared types.
1448      if (SortedSet.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass) || NavigableSet.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1449         return new TreeSet<>();
1450      if (Set.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1451         return new LinkedHashSet<>();
1452      if (Deque.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass) || Queue.class.isAssignableFrom(propertyClass))
1453         return new ArrayDeque<>();
1454      return new ArrayList<>();  // List or raw Collection (and any other collection shape).
1455   }
1456
1457   /**
1458    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this property is write-only.
1459    *
1460    * <p>
1461    * This implies the property MIGHT be readable, but that serializers should not serialize it.
1462    *
1463    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this property is write-only.
1464    */
1465   protected boolean isWriteOnly() { return writeOnly; }
1466
1467   /**
1468    * Sets an array field on this bean.
1469    *
1470    * <p>
1471    * Works on both <c>Object</c> and primitive arrays.
1472    *
1473    * <p>
1474    * <b>Marshalling-only path.</b>  Requires a property built via the marshalling-side construction path
1475    * (i.e. with a non-null {@link #getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta}).  When the owning {@link BeanMeta} was built via
1476    * {@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}, this method throws
1477    * {@link UnsupportedOperationException} because the array element type is only known via {@code rawTypeMeta},
1478    * which is not populated in the bean-modeling-only path.
1479    *
1480    * @param bean The bean of the field.
1481    * @param l The collection to use to set the array field.
1482    * @throws IllegalArgumentException Thrown by method invocation.
1483    * @throws IllegalAccessException Thrown by method invocation.
1484    * @throws InvocationTargetException Thrown by method invocation.
1485    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If this property was built via the bean-modeling-only path
1486    *    ({@link BeanMeta#of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).
1487    */
1488   protected void setArray(Object bean, List l) throws IllegalArgumentException, IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException {
1489      if (rawTypeMeta == null)
1490         throw unsupportedOp("Property ''{0}'' was built via the bean-modeling-only path; setArray requires a marshalling context.", name);
1491      var array = toArray(l, this.rawTypeMeta.getElementType().inner());
1492      invokeSetter(bean, name, array);
1493   }
1494}
